title: Success Stories: Medical Scientist from Nepal in Biomedical Science Secures EB2-NIW Approval, Overcoming RFE Challenge
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
“This is fantastic news! I want to extend my sincere thanks to your entire team for their outstanding assistance with my I-140 application and the subsequent I-140 RFE. I truly believe my case wouldn't have been approved without the dedication and expertise of your professionals.”
On June 16 th , 2025, we received another EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) approval for a Medical Scientist II in the Field of Biomedical Science ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Biomedical Science
Position at the Time of Case Filing : Medical Scientist II
Country of Origin: Nepal
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: Florida
Approval Notice Date: June 16 th , 2025
Processing Time: 5 months and 19 days
At NAILG, we are proud to announce that our client, a medical scientist from Nepal, has successfully secured approval for his I-140 EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) petition, with the premium processing, despite receiving a Request for Evidence (RFE) during the process. After thoroughly reviewing his background and accomplishments in biomedical science, we recognized the national importance and potential of his work, making him a strong candidate for the petition. Our team carefully compiled and presented evidence of his research accomplishments, peer-reviewed publications, and recognition within the field to ensure a compelling case.
Client’s Strong Qualifications and National Impact.
As a medical scientist specializing in biomedical science, the client’s work addresses critical global health concerns, specifically therapeutic interventions for diabetes and liver diseases. His research is poised to significantly impact the understanding and treatment of these diseases, offering solutions to reduce healthcare costs and improve patient outcomes in the U.S.
With funding from prestigious institutions, a strong publication record, and citations reflecting the influence of his work, our client is well-positioned to continue advancing his endeavors. His ongoing contributions to the field promise to further benefit both public health and the medical community.
Key Accomplishments: Demonstrated Significant Recognition.
The petition highlighted several key accomplishments that underscored the client’s impact, including:
● A robust publication record, with 8 peer-reviewed journal articles (5 first-authored), 8 conference abstracts (4 first-authored), and 1 accepted book chapter.
● A total of 25 citations, demonstrating the influence of his research within the biomedical community.
● Peer review experience for prestigious journals, having completed 1 peer review, showcasing his recognition as an expert in his field.
● At least three of his papers rank among the most highly cited in Molecular Biology & Genetics for their respective years of publication.
● Research funding from esteemed institutions, including the National Eye Institute , the National Institute of General Medical Sciences , and the Congressionally Directed Medical Research Program of the Department of Defense.
The petition included four recommendation letters, with two independent advisory opinions from highly regarded experts in the field. The following excerpt from these letters highlighted the innovative nature and national significance of the client’s research, further supporting the petition’s merit:
“[Client’s] leadership in biomedical science is clearly demonstrated by his evaluation of ER stress and its role in diabetic retinopathy. There is thus no doubt that [client] shall continue to produce additional valuable research, both driving progress in the field and bringing immense benefits to national and international concerns.”
Our client received a Request for Evidence (RFE) during the review process. However, through NAILG’s thorough and well-documented response, we successfully addressed all concerns raised by USCIS, which ultimately led to the approval of his EB2- NIW petition.
NAILG’s Strategic Petition Preparation Leads to Approval.
USCIS approved the client’s EB2- NIW petition in just 5 months and 20 days, with the premium processing, a testament to the strength of his qualifications and the national importance of his work in biomedical science. NAILG’s thorough evaluation of the client’s qualifications and strategic petition preparation played a key role in securing the approval of the EB2- NIW petition, despite the RFE.
We congratulate our client on this outstanding achievement and are honored to have supported his continued contributions to advancing medical research and healthcare solutions in the United States.
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
